summary: Good matrix recording of a quirky Spring ’90 show with standout Help>Slip>Franks, Dire Wolf and Iko‑Iko, but a few sloppy moments in the second set.
review: The Miller‑87742 mastering of the Healy board‑and‑mic mix delivers a clear, well‑balanced sound that many fans find superior to the official Cutler mix. Musically the concert is uneven – the first set leans toward Brent’s keyboard‑driven vibe with solid highlights like Dire Wolf and the opening trio, while the second set shines on Help>Slip>Franks, Iko Iko and Attics of My Life before slipping into a sloppy Terrapin and a plodding Standing on the Moon.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews note good recording quality (board‑and‑mic/matrix) but describe an uneven performance with both strong highlights and noticeable low points, leading to a mid‑high rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller.87742 mastering of the Healy board‑and‑mic mix praised as clear and possibly better than the official Cutler mix; energized Tobin matrix adds ambience.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is Brent‑centric and solid, second set starts strong with Iko Iko and an excellent transition into Uncle John's Band, then loses momentum with a sloppy Terrapin and a plodding Standing on the Moon before recovering around Not Fade Away.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Limited fireworks from Jerry in the first set; overall subdued guitar work.
